notes: Three-location dental group. Website looks dated, has many generic service pages, and relies heavily on brand search plus Google Maps traffic.
Boutique cosmetic practice with polished branding, but the blog has not been updated in over a year and mobile booking feels clunky.
Medspa with strong paid social and Instagram presence. Home page is image-heavy and provider-led offers are not clearly segmented by intent.
Membership-led medspa using seasonal promotions. Likely optimized for aesthetic branding more than search clarity or conversion tracking.
Small-group fitness studio pushing free trials. Website appears modern but has oversized hero sections and limited local SEO content.
Pilates studio with strong social community and premium visuals. Likely image-heavy mobile experience with weak conversion pathways from intro offers.
Home services business competing on local search. Service pages are broad, blog posts are basic, and tracking for call leads is unclear.
Clinic with referral traffic and local SEO ambitions. Content exists but reads generic, and above-the-fold messaging may be too clinical and low-conversion.
Commercial cleaning firm. Website likely acts as a brochure with weak trust proof, thin search visibility, and minimal measurement setup.
